items.title: June 9, 2005: A Manu Ginóbili and-1 during the Spurs' 84-69 victory over Detroit in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 9, 2005: A Manu Ginóbili and-1 during the Spurs' 84-69 victory over Detroit in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Ginóbili finished with a game-high 26 PTS (10-16 FG) and 9 REB, and Tim Duncan added 24 PTS/17 REB for San Antonio. Chauncey Billups led the Pistons with 25 PTS/6 AST.

items.title: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend Game 4 of Knicks-Spurs in the NBA Finals on Wednesday night at Madison Square Garden due to scheduling conflicts and obligations, sources tell ESPN. Trump, a lifelong Knicks fan, watched the majority of Game 3 from a suite.

items.title: “It's hard to put your head out of the water."
items.author: Kelly Iko
items.body_excerpt:
“It's hard to put your head out of the water."

In the loudest city in the world, with the pressure of an 0-2 hole threatening their season, Victor Wembanyama did what he does best — found the silence. 

On the man in the Garden, French art and survival instincts.

sports.yahoo.com/nba/article/...

media.title: NBA Finals: Victor Wembanyama finds redemption with Madison Square Garden takeover
media.description: After taking the blame for the Spurs' Game 2 loss and series deficit, Wemby responded the only way he knows how — with cool, calm dominance.
